note_data.title: Nutrition in plants
keyPoints: **Photosynthesis** is the process by which plants manufacture carbohydrates from raw materials using energy from light
**Chlorophyll** absorbs light energy for photosynthesis and is found in chloroplasts
The raw materials for photosynthesis are **carbon dioxide** and **water**
The products of photosynthesis are **glucose** and **oxygen**
**Light intensity**, **carbon dioxide concentration** and **temperature** are limiting factors for photosynthesis
Leaves are adapted for photosynthesis with a large surface area, thin structure and many chloroplasts
**Stomata** allow gas exchange - carbon dioxide enters and oxygen leaves
**Guard cells** control the opening and closing of stomata
Photosynthesis only occurs in the presence of light in chlorophyll-containing cells
The rate of photosynthesis can be measured by counting oxygen bubbles produced by aquatic plants

definitions.definition: The process by which plants manufacture carbohydrates from raw materials using energy from light
Green pigment that absorbs light energy for photosynthesis
A factor that limits the rate of a process when it is in short supply
Pores in the leaf surface that allow gas exchange

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give detailed account of observations or process steps

modelAnswer.question: Explain why leaves are well adapted for photosynthesis. [3 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: Leaves have a large surface area to absorb maximum light. They are thin so carbon dioxide can diffuse quickly to all cells. They contain many chloroplasts with chlorophyll to absorb light energy.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Large surface area to absorb maximum light
Mark 2: Thin structure for rapid diffusion of gases
Mark 3: Many chloroplasts/chlorophyll to absorb light energy

commonMistakes: Never say 'plants breathe' — always say 'gas exchange occurs through stomata'
Students often forget that photosynthesis only occurs in light, not 24 hours
Don't confuse raw materials (CO₂ and water) with products (glucose and oxygen)
Never say 'chloroplasts contain chlorophyll' when asked what chlorophyll is
Students mix up what enters and exits stomata during photosynthesis
examinerTips: Photosynthesis questions appear in every paper - learn the word equation perfectly
Always specify that light energy is needed, not just 'energy'
When describing limiting factors, explain what happens when the factor increases
Draw and label leaf cross-sections clearly for full marks
formulas: carbon dioxide + water → glucose + oxygen (in the presence of light and chlorophyll)
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Cross-section of a leaf showing upper epidermis, palisade mesophyll with chloroplasts, spongy mesophyll, lower epidermis with stomata and guard cells, and vascular bundles
